actionUnits.condition: MonoBehaviour matches Fire (Script GUID 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d). Scene config: debug=0, isOn=0, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, maxNeighborDistance=4 (prefab mod shows 3.5), downMultiplier=2, onNeighborsThreshold=2, minNeighborDownDistance=3, BoxCollider enabled. Precondition for this test: Ensure at least 2 neighbor Fire components within maxNeighborDistance are on (one lower than this fire by >3 units to exercise downMultiplier). Those neighbors must notify this Fire (via their internal UpdateFireState(true) -> NotifyAllNeighbors()). Expected: This Fire sets shouldTurnOn=true, after randomized timeToOn (40-60s) it turns on (isOn=true), plays ParticleSystem, notifies neighbors, and calls FireController.Instance.FireChanged(1).

Event under test: sustained water suppression. Precondition: This Fire is currently on (isOn=true). During the 6s window, it must be hit by water repeatedly such that WaterHit(Vector3 normal) is invoked at intervals < maxHitInterval=2s, accumulating for >= totalHitTime=5s. Expected: isTurningOff stays true, currentHitInterval never exceeds 2s, currentHitTime reaches >=5s, then fire turns off (isOn=false), particle systems stop, neighbors notified, FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1) called, timers reset.

Event under test: cancel turn-off when water hits stop. Precondition: This Fire is on and has just been hit by water once (WaterHit(Vector3) called), which sets isTurningOff=true and resets currentHitInterval=0. Then no further hits for >= maxHitInterval (2s). Expected: currentHitInterval exceeds 2s before totalHitTime, so isTurningOff becomes false and currentHitTime resets without turning off.
